description: Microsoft Exchange OWA edge CVEs — ProxyLogon SSRF (CVE-2021-26855) and ProxyShell path confusion (CVE-2021-34473). Detection-only endpoint fingerprints.

proxylogon_ssrf_2021.description: CVE-2021-26855 (ProxyLogon) — Exchange server-side request forgery via the /owa/ front-end trusting an X-BEResource cookie to reach the back-end Autodiscover, enabling pre-auth impersonation. Detection = confirm the OWA/EWS surface; the SSRF cookie payload is operator-supervised.

probes.description: OWA logon page present (200 with Exchange/OWA markers or X-OWA-Version header) confirms the CVE-2021-26855 ProxyLogon front-end is exposed. Fingerprint only — no X-BEResource SSRF cookie sent.

proxyshell_pathconfusion_2021.description: CVE-2021-34473 (ProxyShell) — Exchange Autodiscover path confusion via the Explicit Logon URL, reaching the PowerShell back-end pre-auth. Detection = confirm autodiscover.json is reachable; no ProxyShell chain is executed.

probes.description: autodiscover.json responding (200/other non-404 with Exchange markers) via the @-in-path Explicit-Logon confusion = CVE-2021-34473 ProxyShell path surface reachable. Detection only — placeholder evil.com tests the path-confusion behaviour, not a callback.
